en.content: Let $y=y(x)$ be the solution of the differential equation $\left(x^2-x \sqrt{x^2-1}\right) d y+\left(y\left(x-\sqrt{x^2-1}\right)-x\right) d x=0, x \geq 1$. If $y(1)=1$, then the greatest integer less than $y(\sqrt{5})$ is $\_\_\_\_$ .
en.explanation:
First write the given differential equation in the standard linear form.

$$\frac{dy}{dx}+\frac{y}{x}=\frac{1}{x-\sqrt{x^2-1}}$$

Now simplify the right side by rationalising:

$$\frac{1}{x-\sqrt{x^2-1}}=\frac{x+\sqrt{x^2-1}}{(x-\sqrt{x^2-1})(x+\sqrt{x^2-1})}=\frac{x+\sqrt{x^2-1}}{x^2-(x^2-1)}=x+\sqrt{x^2-1}$$

So the equation becomes:

$$\frac{dy}{dx}+\frac{y}{x}=x+\sqrt{x^2-1}$$

This is a linear differential equation with integrating factor (I.F.):

I.f $= e^{\ln x}=x$

Multiply the whole equation by $x$:

$$y.x=\int \left(x+\sqrt{x^2-1}\right)x\,dx$$

Now integrate term by term:

$$=\frac{x^2}{2}+\frac{\left(x^2-1\right)^{3/2}}{3}+C$$

Use the initial condition $y(1)=1$ to find $C$.

Given $y(1)=1$

Substitute $x=1$ in the expression for $yx$:

$$1=\frac{1}{2}+C \rightarrow C=\frac{1}{2}$$

So,

$$yx=\frac{x^2}{2}+\frac{(x^2-1)^{3/2}}{3}+\frac{1}{2}$$

Divide by $x$:

$$y=\frac{x}{2}+\frac{(x^2-1)^{3/2}}{3x}+\frac{1}{2x}$$

Now put $x=\sqrt{5}$:

$$\left[y\left(\sqrt{5}\right)\right]=\left[\frac{\sqrt{5}}{2}+\frac{8}{3}+\frac{1}{2\sqrt{5}}\right]=3$$

hi.content: माना अवकल समीकरण $\left(x^2-x \sqrt{x^2-1}\right) d y+\left(y\left(x-\sqrt{x^2-1}\right)-x\right) d x=0, x \geq 1$, का हल $y=y(x)$ है। यदि $y(1)=1$ है, तो $y(\sqrt{5})$ से कम महत्तम पूर्णांक है
hi.explanation:
सबसे पहले दिए गए अवकल समीकरण को मानक रैखिक रूप में लिखें।

$$\frac{dy}{dx}+\frac{y}{x}=\frac{1}{x-\sqrt{x^2-1}}$$

अब परिमेयीकरण करके दाएँ पक्ष को सरल करें:

$$\frac{1}{x-\sqrt{x^2-1}}=\frac{x+\sqrt{x^2-1}}{(x-\sqrt{x^2-1})(x+\sqrt{x^2-1})}=\frac{x+\sqrt{x^2-1}}{x^2-(x^2-1)}=x+\sqrt{x^2-1}$$

इसलिए समीकरण बन जाता है:

$$\frac{dy}{dx}+\frac{y}{x}=x+\sqrt{x^2-1}$$

यह एक रैखिक अवकल समीकरण है जिसका समाकलन गुणक (I.F.) है:

I.F. $= e^{\ln x}=x$

पूरे समीकरण को $x$ से गुणा करें:

$$y.x=\int \left(x+\sqrt{x^2-1}\right)x\,dx$$

अब प्रत्येक पद का समाकलन करें:

$$=\frac{x^2}{2}+\frac{\left(x^2-1\right)^{3/2}}{3}+C$$

$C$ ज्ञात करने के लिए प्रारंभिक शर्त $y(1)=1$ का उपयोग करें।

दिया गया है $y(1)=1$

$yx$ के व्यंजक में $x=1$ प्रतिस्थापित करें:

$$1=\frac{1}{2}+C \rightarrow C=\frac{1}{2}$$

इसलिए,

$$yx=\frac{x^2}{2}+\frac{(x^2-1)^{3/2}}{3}+\frac{1}{2}$$

$x$ से भाग दें:

$$y=\frac{x}{2}+\frac{(x^2-1)^{3/2}}{3x}+\frac{1}{2x}$$

अब $x=\sqrt{5}$ रखें:

$$\left[y\left(\sqrt{5}\right)\right]=\left[\frac{\sqrt{5}}{2}+\frac{8}{3}+\frac{1}{2\sqrt{5}}\right]=3$$

en.content: Let the centre of the circle $x^2+y^2+2 \mathrm{~g} x+2 f y+25=0$ be in the first quadrant and lie on the line $2 x-y=4$. Let the area of an equilateral triangle inscribed in the circle be $27 \sqrt{3}$. Then the square of the length of the chord of the circle on the line $x=1$ is $\_\_\_\_$ .
en.explanation:
For the circle

$$ x^2+y^2+2gx+2fy+25=0, $$

the centre is

$$ (-g,-f) $$

and radius is

$$ r=\sqrt{g^2+f^2-25}. $$

We are given that the centre lies in the first quadrant, so

$$ (-g,-f) $$

is in the first quadrant.

Also, the centre lies on the line

$$ 2x-y=4. $$

So if the centre is $(-g,-f)$, then

$$ 2(-g)-(-f)=4 $$

that is,

$$ -2g+f=4. $$

Now use the information about the equilateral triangle inscribed in the circle.

For an equilateral triangle of side $a$,

$$ \text{Area}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}a^2. $$

Given area is

$$ 27\sqrt{3}. $$

So,

$$ \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}a^2=27\sqrt{3} $$

which gives

$$ a^2=108 \quad \Rightarrow \quad a=6\sqrt{3}. $$

For an equilateral triangle inscribed in a circle, the circumradius is

$$ R=\frac{a}{\sqrt{3}}. $$

Hence,

$$ r=\frac{6\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{3}}=6. $$

Therefore,

$$ r^2=36. $$

So,

$$ g^2+f^2-25=36 $$

which gives

$$ g^2+f^2=61. $$

Now solve the system

$$ -2g+f=4 $$

and

$$ g^2+f^2=61. $$

From the first equation,

$$ f=4+2g. $$

Substitute into the second:

$$ g^2+(4+2g)^2=61 $$

$$ g^2+16+16g+4g^2=61 $$

$$ 5g^2+16g-45=0. $$

Solve:

$$ 5g^2+16g-45=0. $$

Discriminant:

$$ \Delta=16^2-4\cdot 5\cdot(-45)=256+900=1156=34^2. $$

Thus,

$$ g=\frac{-16\pm 34}{10}. $$

So,

$$ g=\frac{18}{10}=\frac95 \quad \text{or} \quad g=\frac{-50}{10}=-5. $$

Now centre is $(-g,-f)$ and must be in the first quadrant.

If $g=\frac95$, then

$$ f=4+2\cdot \frac95=\frac{38}{5}, $$

so centre is

$$ \left(-\frac95,-\frac{38}{5}\right), $$

not in first quadrant.

Hence reject.

If $g=-5$, then

$$ f=4+2(-5)=-6. $$

So centre is

$$ (5,6), $$

which is in the first quadrant.

Thus the circle is

$$ x^2+y^2-10x-12y+25=0, $$

with centre $(5,6)$ and radius $6$.

Now we need the chord cut by the line

$$ x=1. $$

Distance of the line $x=1$ from the centre $(5,6)$ is

$$ |5-1|=4. $$

If a chord is at distance $d$ from the centre in a circle of radius $r$, then chord length is

$$ 2\sqrt{r^2-d^2}. $$

So here chord length is

$$ 2\sqrt{6^2-4^2}=2\sqrt{36-16}=2\sqrt{20}=4\sqrt{5}. $$

Therefore, the square of the chord length is

$$ (4\sqrt{5})^2=80. $$

Hence, the required answer is

$$ \boxed{80}. $$

hi.content: माना वृत्त $x^2+y^2+2 g x+2 f y+25=0$ का केन्द्र प्रथम चतुर्थांश में है और रेखा $2 x-y=4$ पर है। माना वृत्त के अंतर्गत एक समबाहु त्रिभुज का क्षेत्रफल $27 \sqrt{3}$ है। तो रेखा $x=1$ पर वृत्त की जीवा की लंबाई का वर्ग है $\_\_\_\_$ I

en.content: For the functions $f(\theta)=\alpha \tan ^2 \theta+\beta \cot ^2 \theta$, and $g(\theta)=\alpha \sin ^2 \theta+\beta \cos ^2 \theta, \alpha>\beta>0$, let $\min\limits_{0<\theta<\frac{\pi}{2}} f(\theta)=\max\limits_{0<\theta<\pi} g(\theta)$. If the first term of a G.P. is $\left(\frac{\alpha}{2 \beta}\right)$, its common ratio is $\left(\frac{2 \beta}{\alpha}\right)$ and the sum of its first 10 terms is $\frac{m}{n}, \operatorname{gcd}(m, n)=1$, then $m+n$ is equal to $\_\_\_\_$ .
en.explanation:
For

$$ f(\theta)=\alpha \tan^2\theta+\beta \cot^2\theta $$

and

$$ g(\theta)=\alpha \sin^2\theta+\beta \cos^2\theta, \qquad \alpha>\beta>0 $$

we are given that

$$ \min_{0<\theta<\frac{\pi}{2}} f(\theta)=\max_{0<\theta<\pi} g(\theta). $$

We will first find these two values.

1. Minimum value of $f(\theta)$

Let

$$ x=\tan^2\theta. $$

Since $0<\theta<\frac{\pi}{2}$, we have $x>0$.

Then

$$ \cot^2\theta=\frac{1}{\tan^2\theta}=\frac{1}{x}. $$

So

$$ f(\theta)=\alpha x+\frac{\beta}{x}. $$

Now use the standard result for $x>0$:

$$ ax+\frac{b}{x} \ge 2\sqrt{ab}. $$

Hence,

$$ \alpha x+\frac{\beta}{x}\ge 2\sqrt{\alpha\beta}. $$

Therefore,

$$ \min f(\theta)=2\sqrt{\alpha\beta}. $$

2. Maximum value of $g(\theta)$

We have

$$ g(\theta)=\alpha \sin^2\theta+\beta \cos^2\theta. $$

Using

$$ \cos^2\theta=1-\sin^2\theta, $$

we get

$$ g(\theta)=\alpha \sin^2\theta+\beta(1-\sin^2\theta) $$

$$ = \beta+(\alpha-\beta)\sin^2\theta. $$

Since $\alpha>\beta$, the coefficient of $\sin^2\theta$ is positive. So $g(\theta)$ is maximum when

$$ \sin^2\theta=1. $$

For $0<\theta<\pi$, this happens at $\theta=\frac{\pi}{2}$.

Thus,

$$ \max g(\theta)=\beta+(\alpha-\beta)=\alpha. $$

3. Use the given condition

Given

$$ \min f(\theta)=\max g(\theta), $$

so

$$ 2\sqrt{\alpha\beta}=\alpha. $$

Now square both sides:

$$ 4\alpha\beta=\alpha^2. $$

Since $\alpha>0$, divide by $\alpha$:

$$ 4\beta=\alpha. $$

So,

$$ \alpha=4\beta. $$

4. G.P. data

First term:

$$ a=\frac{\alpha}{2\beta}=\frac{4\beta}{2\beta}=2. $$

Common ratio:

$$ r=\frac{2\beta}{\alpha}=\frac{2\beta}{4\beta}=\frac{1}{2}. $$

So the G.P. is

$$ 2,\ 1,\ \frac12,\ \frac14,\dots $$

5. Sum of first 10 terms

For a G.P.,

$$ S_n=\frac{a(1-r^n)}{1-r}, \qquad r\ne 1. $$

Thus,

$$ S_{10}=\frac{2\left(1-\left(\frac12\right)^{10}\right)}{1-\frac12}. $$

Now simplify:

$$ S_{10}=2\cdot \frac{1-\frac{1}{1024}}{\frac12} $$

$$ =2\cdot 2\left(1-\frac{1}{1024}\right) $$

$$ =4\cdot \frac{1023}{1024} $$

$$ =\frac{4092}{1024} $$

$$ =\frac{1023}{256}. $$

So,

$$ \frac{m}{n}=\frac{1023}{256}. $$

Hence,

$$ m+n=1023+256=1279. $$

Therefore, the answer is

$$ \boxed{1279}. $$

$$ S_{10}=\frac{2\left(1-\left(\frac12\right)^{10}\right)}{1-\frac12}. $$

Now simplify:

$$ S_{10}=2\cdot \frac{1-\frac{1}{1024}}{\frac12} $$

$$ =2\cdot 2\left(1-\frac{1}{1024}\right) $$

$$ =4\cdot \frac{1023}{1024} $$

$$ =\frac{4092}{1024} $$

$$ =\frac{1023}{256}. $$

So,

$$ \frac{m}{n}=\frac{1023}{256}. $$

Hence,

$$ m+n=1023+256=1279. $$

Therefore, the answer is

$$ \boxed{1279}. $$

en.content: If $\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}, \vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k}$ and $\vec{c}$ be three vectors such that $\vec{a} \times \vec{c}=\vec{b}$ and $\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}=3$, then $\vec{c} \cdot(\vec{a}-2 \vec{b})$ is equal to $\_\_\_\_$ .
en.explanation:
Let

$$ \vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}, \qquad \vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k} $$

We have to find

$$ \vec{c}\cdot (\vec{a}-2\vec{b}) $$

given that

$$ \vec{a}\times \vec{c}=\vec{b} \quad \text{and} \quad \vec{a}\cdot \vec{c}=3 $$

Let

$$ \vec{c}=x\hat{i}+y\hat{j}+z\hat{k} $$

Then

$$ \vec{a}=(1,1,1), \qquad \vec{b}=(0,1,-1) $$

Now compute $\vec{a}\times \vec{c}$:

$$ \vec{a}\times \vec{c} = \begin{vmatrix} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k}\\ 1 & 1 & 1\\ x & y & z \end{vmatrix} $$

Expanding,

$$ \vec{a}\times \vec{c} = \hat{i}(z-y)-\hat{j}(z-x)+\hat{k}(y-x) $$

Since this is equal to $\vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k}$, we compare components:

$$ z-y=0 $$

$$ -(z-x)=1 \Rightarrow x-z=1 $$

$$ y-x=-1 $$

Also, from $\vec{a}\cdot \vec{c}=3$,

$$ x+y+z=3 $$

Now solve the equations.

From $z-y=0$,

$$ z=y $$

From $x-z=1$,

$$ x=y+1 $$

Substitute into $x+y+z=3$:

$$ (y+1)+y+y=3 $$

$$ 3y+1=3 $$

$$ 3y=2 \Rightarrow y=\frac{2}{3} $$

So,

$$ z=\frac{2}{3}, \qquad x=\frac{5}{3} $$

Hence,

$$ \vec{c}=\frac{5}{3}\hat{i}+\frac{2}{3}\hat{j}+\frac{2}{3}\hat{k} $$

Now find $\vec{a}-2\vec{b}$:

$$ \vec{a}-2\vec{b} = (\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k})-2(\hat{j}-\hat{k}) $$

$$ = \hat{i}-\hat{j}+3\hat{k} $$

Therefore,

$$ \vec{c}\cdot(\vec{a}-2\vec{b}) = \left(\frac{5}{3},\frac{2}{3},\frac{2}{3}\right)\cdot(1,-1,3) $$

$$ = \frac{5}{3}-\frac{2}{3}+2 $$

$$ = \frac{3}{3}+2=1+2=3 $$

So, the required value is

$$ \boxed{3} $$

hi.content: यदि तीन सदिश $\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}, \vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k}$ और $\vec{c}$ इस प्रकार हैं कि $\vec{a} \times \vec{c}=\vec{b}$ और $\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}=3$ हैं, तो $\vec{c} \cdot(\vec{a}-2 \vec{b})$ बराबर है $\_\_\_\_$
hi.explanation:
माना

$$ \vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}, \qquad \vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k} $$

हमें ज्ञात करना है

$$ \vec{c}\cdot (\vec{a}-2\vec{b}) $$

दिया गया है कि

$$ \vec{a}\times \vec{c}=\vec{b} \quad \text{और} \quad \vec{a}\cdot \vec{c}=3 $$

माना

$$ \vec{c}=x\hat{i}+y\hat{j}+z\hat{k} $$

तब

$$ \vec{a}=(1,1,1), \qquad \vec{b}=(0,1,-1) $$

अब $\vec{a}\times \vec{c}$ की गणना करें:

$$ \vec{a}\times \vec{c} = \begin{vmatrix} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k}\\ 1 & 1 & 1\\ x & y & z \end{vmatrix} $$

विस्तार करने पर,

$$ \vec{a}\times \vec{c} = \hat{i}(z-y)-\hat{j}(z-x)+\hat{k}(y-x) $$

चूँकि यह $\vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k}$ के बराबर है, हम घटकों की तुलना करते हैं:

$$ z-y=0 $$

$$ -(z-x)=1 \Rightarrow x-z=1 $$

$$ y-x=-1 $$

साथ ही, $\vec{a}\cdot \vec{c}=3$ से,

$$ x+y+z=3 $$

अब समीकरणों को हल करते हैं।

$z-y=0$ से,

$$ z=y $$

$x-z=1$ से,

$$ x=y+1 $$

$x+y+z=3$ में प्रतिस्थापित करने पर:

$$ (y+1)+y+y=3 $$

$$ 3y+1=3 $$

$$ 3y=2 \Rightarrow y=\frac{2}{3} $$

इसलिए,

$$ z=\frac{2}{3}, \qquad x=\frac{5}{3} $$

अतः,

$$ \vec{c}=\frac{5}{3}\hat{i}+\frac{2}{3}\hat{j}+\frac{2}{3}\hat{k} $$

अब $\vec{a}-2\vec{b}$ ज्ञात करते हैं:

$$ \vec{a}-2\vec{b} = (\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k})-2(\hat{j}-\hat{k}) $$

$$ = \hat{i}-\hat{j}+3\hat{k} $$

इसलिए,

$$ \vec{c}\cdot(\vec{a}-2\vec{b}) = \left(\frac{5}{3},\frac{2}{3},\frac{2}{3}\right)\cdot(1,-1,3) $$

$$ = \frac{5}{3}-\frac{2}{3}+2 $$

$$ = \frac{3}{3}+2=1+2=3 $$

अतः, अभीष्ट मान है

$$ \boxed{3} $$
